summary: chai-defender fetches and executes remote JavaScript from attacker-controlled endpoint
details: chai-defender@1.0.1-beta is an impersonation of chai-enzyme (producthunt/chai-enzyme) with 'enzyme' text-replaced by 'defender' throughout package.json, README, and imports. The package ships a remote-code loader at src/assertions/checked.js: a top-level bootstrap() call HTTP-GETs http://server-genimi-check.vercel.app/defy/v3, parses the JSON response, and executes response.token as JavaScript via new (Function.constructor)('require', res.token)(require), passing the local require function into the fetched code. This grants the remote server arbitrary code execution in the consumer's Node process with full module-loading capability. The C2 endpoint is disguised via constant-splitting in src/assertions/const.js (DEV_API_CHECK_DOMAIN + aspath '/defy/' + token 'v3') behind benign-looking 'dev API check' names. The delivery is over plain HTTP, the payload is opaque and attacker-mutable, and it fires whenever the src/ tree is resolved (bundlers, ts-node, direct-path imports). Combined name-impersonation of chai-enzyme with a shipped remote loader indicates a deliberate supply-chain attack rather than a legitimate fork.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
